title: Avoid octal number literals
description: Octal literals starting with zero can be easily mistaken for decimal values by developers unfamiliar with octal notation. This can lead to unexpected behavior and logic errors when the intended decimal value differs from the octal interpretation.
recommendation: Use decimal or explicitly marked hexadecimal literals instead of octal notation.

noncompliant.description: Octal literals starting with 0 can be misread as decimal values, leading to logical errors.

compliant.description: Using decimal values or explicit constants makes the code's intent clear and prevents misinterpretation.

checks.message: Avoid octal number literals - use decimal or hexadecimal instead
